author: phase-4-coordinator
content:
Implemented via new `*_axes` variants that take axes: Option<&[usize]> and keepdims: bool. The existing single-axis sum/prod/min/max/mean/var/std_ stay unchanged — this is non-breaking. See #458 for the companion multi-axis work; both issues shipped together.

Added functions in ferray-stats/src/reductions/mod.rs:
- sum_axes, prod_axes, min_axes, max_axes, mean_axes, var_axes, std_axes — all accept axes: Option<&[usize]> (None or &[] = reduce all) plus keepdims
- argmin_keepdims, argmax_keepdims — single-axis + keepdims (NumPy's argmin/argmax don't accept multi-axis)

New internal helpers:
- normalize_axes: handles None / empty slice / validates in-bounds / rejects duplicates / sorts
- output_shape_axes: computes the output shape with or without keepdims
- reduce_axes_general: multi-axis reduction primitive that partitions axes into keep/reduce sets and iterates the full Cartesian product
- reduce_axes_general_u64: same for u64 outputs (argmin/argmax)

Closing as completed.
